opp.name: Angelone_S2P_Jun'26
opp.next_step: Next Step - Meeting scheduled for 2nd July for deeper discussion 30th June - received the Business Requirement Document. Potential S2P opportunity identified, we're in touch with the Head of Technology Operations.
opp.ais_why:
Top management at Angel One has explicitly set an expectation that the new procurement platform must have a credible AI roadmap. This is not a "nice to have" — it is a qualification criterion for vendors. 
Pradeep and the team expressed disappointment that their existing system never received meaningful feature updates (e.g., no OCR capability was added over time). This has created a strong appetite for a modern, AI-forward platform.

items.quote: No financial impact, ROI, or savings metrics captured in any Salesforce field or call

items.quote: No budget authority or executive sponsor mapped; only Head of Technology Operations (Decision Maker) in Contact Roles

sources: Next Step field (30 June 2026): BRD received, meeting scheduled 2 July
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI metrics have been captured. The Next Step field references a 'potential S2P opportunity' and receipt of a Business Requirement Document on 30 June, but no Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) contains financial impact, headcount savings, cycle-time reduction, or error-rate improvement. No Avoma calls are available to extract buyer-stated quantified pain. The scheduled 2 July meeting is the immediate opportunity to capture the business problem in the buyer's words and anchor it to quantified impact (cost, time, compliance risk, manual effort). Until metrics are extracted, the value proposition remains unanchored and the deal cannot progress credibly past discovery.

sources: Contact Roles: Decision Maker (Head of Technology Operations), no Champion role
Avoma: no calls available to assess advocacy behavior
champion.narrative: No champion is identified. The Head of Technology Operations holds a Decision Maker contact role, but there is no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or access to the Economic Buyer. A Decision Maker role alone does not constitute a champion unless they are actively pushing Zycus forward, introducing us to budget authority, and defending our position against competitors. No Salesforce field (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) names a champion, and no Avoma calls show advocacy behavior. The deal is currently single-threaded through one technical stakeholder. Champion development is the highest-priority non-technical move: identify who inside Angel One will sell Zycus when we are not in the room, secure their commitment, and map their path to the Economic Buyer.

sources: Next Step, Tasks, Avoma: no competitive mentions
competition.narrative: No competitors are identified. The Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty, and no free-text field (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c) mentions a rival vendor or incumbent solution. No Avoma calls, Next Step entries, or Tasks reference competitive benchmarking. This is unusual for an S2P deal with a BRD in hand—most enterprise buyers at this stage are comparing 2-4 vendors or have an incumbent they are looking to replace. The 2 July meeting and BRD review should surface whether this is a sole-source evaluation, a competitive RFP, or a replacement scenario, and which vendors (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua, Sievo, or an incumbent legacy system) are in play. Until competition is known, we cannot position differentiation or deploy the wedge.

sources: Next Step (30 June 2026): 'potential S2P opportunity', no pain detail
identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain has been captured. The Next Step field describes a 'potential S2P opportunity' and notes contact with the Head of Technology Operations, but no Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) documents what is broken in the current state, the cost of inaction, or the pain owner. No Avoma calls exist to quote buyer-articulated pain. Without identified pain, there is no anchor for urgency, no compelling event to drive the June 2027 timeline, and no clear pain owner to champion the deal internally. The 2 July meeting is the moment to extract and document the pain in the buyer's words, tie it to a business impact, and name the executive who owns it.

sources: Next Step: no mention of contracting or legal
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, and InfoSec requirements are completely unknown. No Salesforce field addresses MSA/SOW status, legal review, or security/compliance gates. No SI or partner involvement is documented. With a June 2027 close date and a $100k S2P deal spanning nine products (including Merlin/Agentic AI, which may trigger additional data-privacy and integration scrutiny), the paper process can add 60-90 days to the cycle. The 2 July discovery meeting and subsequent BRD review should surface whether Angel One requires InfoSec review, legal redlines, a master services agreement, or implementation scoping with a partner. Without this visibility, the close-date forecast is at risk.

sources: Contact Roles: Decision Maker mapped (Head of Technology Operations), no EB or Executive Sponsor role
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified. The only mapped stakeholder is the Head of Technology Operations (Decision Maker role per Contact Role). No Salesforce field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) names a budget authority, and no Avoma calls exist to reveal who controls commercial decisions or pricing conversations. In a $100k S2P deal at an enterprise account, the Economic Buyer is typically a CFO, Chief Procurement Officer, or senior finance/operations executive. The 2 July meeting with the Head of Technology Operations should surface the approval chain and the executive sponsor who will sign off on the investment. Without EB identification, commercials and pricing conversations lack an anchor, and forecast confidence is at risk.

sources: Next Step (30 June 2026): BRD received, meeting scheduled
Contact Roles: single Decision Maker, no multi-threading visible
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. No Salesforce field (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c) describes the buyer's procurement timeline, approval gates, or stakeholder sign-off sequence. Only one contact (Head of Technology Operations, Decision Maker) is mapped, so the broader buying committee and escalation path are invisible. The BRD delivery and scheduled meeting suggest a formal process is underway, but we do not know whether this is an RFI, RFP, sole-source evaluation, or vendor shortlist scenario, nor the timeline for shortlisting, evaluation, and selection. The 2 July meeting should extract the full process map: stakeholders, gates, decision date, and any partner/SI involvement.

sources: Next Step (30 June 2026): 'received the Business Requirement Document'
Salesforce criteria fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c): empty
decision_criteria.narrative: A Business Requirement Document was delivered by the buyer on 30 June 2026, signaling that formal evaluation criteria exist and the buyer is running a structured procurement process. However, the content of the BRD and the specific decision criteria (functional requirements, integration needs, AI/Merlin appetite, pricing/commercial model preferences, compliance/security mandates) have not yet been extracted into Salesforce or captured on a call. The 2 July meeting is the moment to walk through the BRD, confirm the weighted criteria, and map Zycus capabilities to their scoring model. Until the criteria are documented, we cannot position differentiation or de-risk evaluation gaps.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Angel One Ltd — Qualified · $100K · closes 30 Jun

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 1 reflects a weak position: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, commercial). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 40 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 2 open commitment(s); Customer shared internal process / commitments.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (1), nudged by commitment 40 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI product scope is in play (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA) but no AIS score, status, or buyer-stated AI appetite is captured. Without a call or AIS signal, AI positioning strength cannot be assessed.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 30 June 2026, immediately qualified same day. Buyer delivered a Business Requirement Document on 30 June. Meeting scheduled for 2 July per Next Step. LastActivityDate 2 July confirms ongoing engagement. No stage progression yet; deal is 2 days old and appropriately in early Qualified stage.

items.action: Extract and document the quantified business case from the 2 July BRD discussion: financial impact (cost, savings, ROI), operational pain (cycle time, error rate, manual effort), and compelling event driving the June 2027 timeline. Anchor the pain to a named executive pain owner.
items.trigger: BRD delivered 30 June; meeting scheduled 2 July; no quantified metrics captured in Salesforce
items.expected_effect: Establishes urgency, executive selling power, and ROI anchor for commercials; enables credible forecast progression

items.action: Map the Economic Buyer and executive sponsor during the 2 July meeting: ask the Head of Technology Operations who controls the S2P budget, who signs off on the investment, and who the internal business case will be presented to. Secure an introduction or include them in the next stakeholder meeting.
items.trigger: Only Decision Maker (Head of Technology Operations) mapped; no EB or executive sponsor in Contact Roles or Salesforce fields
items.expected_effect: De-risks forecast by surfacing budget authority; enables commercial and pricing conversations with the right stakeholder

items.action: Extract the BRD decision criteria and map to Zycus capabilities: walk through the weighted evaluation criteria (functional requirements, integration needs, AI appetite, pricing model, compliance/security mandates) and document where Zycus is strong, at parity, or exposed. Surface any gaps early.
items.trigger: BRD received 30 June but content not yet extracted into Salesforce; Decision_Criteria and Business_Requirements fields empty
items.expected_effect: Enables targeted positioning and de-risks evaluation stage; surfaces gaps before they become blockers

items.action: Surface the decision process, approval gates, and competitive landscape during the 2 July meeting: ask whether Angel One is running an RFI/RFP, how many vendors are being evaluated, what the shortlist and selection timeline looks like, and what approval sign-offs are required (finance, legal, InfoSec, procurement).
items.trigger: Decision process, competition, and paper process completely unknown; process and competitor fields empty
items.expected_effect: Reveals competitive positioning needs, timeline realism, and contracting/legal lead time; enables process-aligned moves

items.action: Develop multi-threading and identify a champion: ask the Head of Technology Operations to introduce the Zycus team to the procurement lead, finance stakeholder, or operational executive who will benefit from the S2P transformation. Assess who inside Angel One will advocate for Zycus when the team is not in the room, and cultivate that relationship.
items.trigger: Single-threaded through one Decision Maker; no champion with advocacy behavior; no multi-threading to finance/procurement/executive layer
items.expected_effect: De-risks single-point-of-contact dependency; secures internal selling and EB access; accelerates stakeholder alignment

confidence_signals.summary: Fresh engagement: LastActivityDate 2 July (0 days ago), meeting scheduled for 2 July, BRD delivered 30 June. Buyer is actively participating. However, deal is 2 days old, no Avoma calls exist, and all MEDDPICC elements except Decision Maker are unknown. Engagement is live but insight density is very low.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 363. Deal age: 2 days since creation (2026-06-30). Time in Qualified stage: approximately 2 days. No prior stage progression data available. No forward slip observed. With a June 2027 close date, the deal has ample runway for the 12-15 month enterprise motion. Current pace is appropriate for early Qualified stage where discovery and BRD review are underway. Buyer delivered a Business Requirement Document on 30 June and a meeting is scheduled for 2 July, demonstrating active buyer participation.
evidence: Rep-initiated outreach: meeting scheduled for 2 July per Next Step
Buyer delivered BRD on 30 June 2026 (Next Step)
Decision Maker role mapped: Head of Technology Operations (Contact Role)
Broad product scope confirmed: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA (Salesforce Products field)
Deal created 2026-06-30, immediately qualified, demonstrating fast initial traction
north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage S2P opportunity with fresh engagement and strong product scope; Decision Maker mapped but Economic Buyer, champion, and quantified business case remain unknown.

Decision Maker mapped but Economic Buyer, champion, quantified pain, decision process, and competition are unknown. The 2 July meeting is the critical moment to extract these fundamentals from the BRD conversation and establish multi-threading.

competitive_position.summary: No competitors identified across any source. Salesforce Competitors__c field is empty, and no free-text fields, Next Step entries, Tasks, or Avoma calls mention rival vendors or an incumbent solution. This is a knowledge gap for an enterprise S2P deal with a BRD in hand. The 2 July meeting and BRD review should surface whether Angel One is running a competitive RFP, replacing an incumbent, or sole-sourcing to Zycus.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Strong AI product scope confirmed (Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, ANA in Salesforce Products field) but no AIS score, no buyer-stated AI appetite in calls, and no evidence the Merlin/agentic narrative has been positioned. The product scope suggests the rep has planted the AI seed, but without a call transcript or AIS signal we cannot assess whether the buyer is AI Hungry, AI Curious, or indifferent. The 2 July meeting should surface AI appetite explicitly and position Merlin negotiation intelligence as a Phase 1 or Phase 2 anchor.

items.criterion: Broad S2P suite (sourcing, contracting, procurement, supplier, risk, intake)

items.quote: Merlin;Merlin Intake;Agentic AI;ANA in product scope

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope is broad and confirmed: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA. This signals a comprehensive S2P transformation ambition spanning sourcing, contracting, supplier management, procurement, risk, intake, and AI-driven automation. The inclusion of Merlin and Agentic AI suggests appetite for intelligent automation and negotiation support. However, the BRD content, specific functional requirements, and weighted evaluation criteria have not been extracted, so alignment between buyer needs and Zycus capabilities cannot yet be assessed. The 2 July meeting is the moment to map the BRD requirements to our product roadmap and surface any gaps.

hard.ais_why:
Top management at Angel One has explicitly set an expectation that the new procurement platform must have a credible AI roadmap. This is not a "nice to have" — it is a qualification criterion for vendors. 
Pradeep and the team expressed disappointment that their existing system never received meaningful feature updates (e.g., no OCR capability was added over time). This has created a strong appetite for a modern, AI-forward platform.
